supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on independent agents and brokers for distribution, technology systems for business operations, and reinsurance capacity for catastrophe risk management
risks: Dependence on independent agents and brokers, technology system failures, reinsurance counterparty credit risk, third-party vendor performance risks, cloud-based technology vulnerabilities
Cybersecurity risks, technology system failures, reliance on third-party service providers

regulatory_risks: Changes to workers' compensation laws
Rate and policy form approval requirements by state regulators
State insurance regulation changes may reduce profitability and limit growth
Risk-based capital (RBC) requirements and potential increases
Legislative expansion of liability coverage or removal of tort reforms
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance in international operations
Potential changes to Terrorism Risk Insurance Program (authorized through December 31, 2027)
Data privacy and protection laws (biometric data laws, GDPR implications)
Climate change regulations potentially increasing costs for policyholders
Regulatory responses to catastrophe events affecting underwriting flexibility
Potential federal regulation expansion (Federal Insurance Office, FSOC SIFI designation)
Group Capital Calculation (GCC) requirements
Vaccine mandate related claims
Changes in tax laws including potential changes to U.S. Federal budget and tax regulations
Dividend limitations on insurance subsidiaries by state regulators
OECD base erosion and profit shifting rules with global minimum tax of 15%
State laws mandating discounts for risk mitigation practices
State insurance regulations restricting ability to cancel or non-renew policies in catastrophe-prone areas
Regulatory restrictions on capital requirements and surplus levels
Cannabis legalization impact on automobile accidents
Insurance holding company laws limiting dividend payments from subsidiaries
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program expiration (scheduled December 31, 2027)
Cyber-security regulations (NY DFS Cybersecurity Requirements, Insurance Data Security Model Law)
State guaranty fund and insurance-related assessments
International regulatory requirements (Solvency II in EU, PRA/FCA in UK, OSFI in Canada)
Residual market mechanisms and guaranty fund assessments
Climate change disclosure requirements (CSRD, CSDDD in EU)
Tax law changes (premium taxes, federal corporate taxes, investment tax exemptions)
Potential repeal of McCarran-Ferguson Act
Restrictions on exiting geographic markets or canceling/nonrenewing policies
Restrictions on underwriting criteria (credit scoring, use of external data, AI/algorithms)

sanction_risks: Geopolitical tensions increasing cyber-attack risks from nation-states
International trade regulation changes including tariffs could lead to inflation
Political instability in foreign markets causing financial market disruption
